verses.text: David cries for help, for godly and faithful men seem to fail, while people speak emptiness and flattery with double hearts. Truth grows rare in evil days. When honesty is scarce, the believer turns to God, who never fails.

David cries for help, for godly and faithful men seem to fail, while people speak emptiness and flattery with double hearts. Truth grows rare in evil days. When honesty is scarce, the believer turns to God, who never fails.

He trusts God to cut off the flattering lips of those who boast, 'our lips are our own: who is lord over us?' Proud speech defies all authority. But the tongue that owns no master will answer to the Lord of all.

He trusts God to cut off the flattering lips of those who boast, 'our lips are our own: who is lord over us?' Proud speech defies all authority. But the tongue that owns no master will answer to the Lord of all.

'For the oppression of the poor... now will I arise, saith the LORD,' and His own words are pure, like silver refined seven times. God hears the groaning of the needy. His promises, unlike men's flattery, are perfectly trustworthy.

'For the oppression of the poor... now will I arise, saith the LORD,' and His own words are pure, like silver refined seven times. God hears the groaning of the needy. His promises, unlike men's flattery, are perfectly trustworthy.

David is sure the LORD will preserve His own, though the wicked strut about when vile men are honoured. Evil seems to flourish for a time. Yet God keeps His people safe even in a world that exalts the base.

David is sure the LORD will preserve His own, though the wicked strut about when vile men are honoured. Evil seems to flourish for a time. Yet God keeps His people safe even in a world that exalts the base.
